entries.definition:
kh–d–d cheek, pillow, lateral side; furrow, groove, ditch,
trench, to furrow, (of a flood) to cut a channel, to become divided
into factions. Of this root, two forms occur once each in the
Qur’an: خَدّ khadd and أُخْدُود ʾukhdūd.

خَدّ khadd [n.] cheek *(31:18) خَدَّكَ وَلَا تُصَعِّرْ to be arrogant, to treat
arrogantly [lit. to turn your cheek towards someone/away].

أُخْدُود ʾukhdūd [n.] a trench, ditch, furrow (85:4) قُتِلَ أَصْحَابُ الْأُخْدُودِ
perish the people of the trench!; * أصحاب الأخدود the people of the
trench, said by ʾibn ʾisḥāq to refer to Jewish convert, King Dhū
Nuwās, and some people of Najrān who persecuted Christians
there by burning them in a trench (see ج/ر/ب b–r–j). This
provoked the Christian Abyssinians to conquer southern Arabia,
and to attack the Kaʿba in the ‘Year of the Elephant’ when the
Prophet Muḥammad was born (see ب/ح/ص ṣ–ḥ–b).
